stories.headline: US says it has agreed deal with Iran to halt strikes and resume talks
stories.summary: Tehran yet to confirm end of tit-for-tat attacks that threaten fragile truce in Strait of Hormuz

stories.headline: US says it has agreed to 'stand down' after exchange of strikes with Iran

stories.headline: South Korea Bets $880B on AI Chips & Data Centers | The Pulse 6/29/2026
stories.summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Marieke Blom, ING Chief Economist and Global Head of Research; Eirik Lie, Kongsberg CEO; Jeremy Allaire, Circle CEO; Frank Smets, Bank for International Settlements Act

stories.headline: US and Iran Agree to Halt Attacks Ahead of Talks
stories.summary:
The US and Iran have agreed to stop attacking each other before peace talks resume this week over the Strait of Hormuz and other issues.

It follows days of tit-for-tat attacks that risk slowing progress toward restoring traffic through the vital waterway. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Kongsberg Has ‘Really Steep’ Demand for Its Missiles, CEO Says
stories.summary: Kongsberg has seen a "positive movement forward" in European defense spending throughout last year, even though it’s still not yet at the level needed, its CEO Eirik Lie tells Bloomberg TV. Lie spoke to Bloomberg TV's Francine Lacqua ahead of July's NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey, where European NATO states will be expected to prove they are progressing towards spending 5% of GDP on defense. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Three Firefighters Die as Wildfires Ravage Utah and Colorado
stories.summary: Fires are raging in the arid Southwest after a warm winter. Forecasters say the fires may grow rapidly, and parts of Colorado have been ordered to evacuate.

stories.headline: Israel triggers huge explosion in southern Lebanon

stories.headline: Australia and Vanuatu sign deal to block foreign military bases
stories.summary: Two countries sign economic and security pact, as China expresses concern that the agreement may be targeted at it.

stories.headline: South Korean president, ex-players, fans demand change after World Cup exit

stories.headline: Vanuatu, Australia ink deal to bar foreign military bases in Pacific nation
The AI boom propping up markets could trigger the next crash, central banks warn
stories.summary: The vast surge of investment in AI, which has powered global stock markets to record highs, risks ending in a financial bust, the Bank for International Settlements warns, as the build-up’s hidden costs begin to surface in company accounts and consumer prices alike.

stories.headline: Starmer resigns: Which European country has the highest turnover of leaders?
stories.summary: The resignation of Keir Starmer means that the UK, a European country once known for its ability to return stable governments, will see its seventh prime minister since 2016. Social media users claim this is the highest rate on the continent. Is this true?

stories.headline: PBOC’s New Overnight Rate Said Below Forecasts in Hint at Easing
stories.summary: China’s central bank set the interest rate on its new overnight liquidity tool at a level that was below expectations, according to people familiar with the matter, in what some economists see as a de facto rate cut that could push down market borrowing costs.

stories.headline: Godiva’s Japan Lenders Agree on Nine-Month Loan Extension
stories.summary: Lenders to Godiva Japan Inc. have agreed to extend by nine months the maturity of about ¥75 billion ($463 million) in loans to the struggling chocolate maker, people familiar with the matter said.

stories.headline: Nigerian Government Seeks Advisers for Planned Eurobond Offering
stories.summary: Nigeria invited expressions of interest from advisers for a planned Eurobond sale, the first since a November issuance drew five times more demand than the amount on offer.

stories.headline: Booming Bank Capital Markets Hand Weakest Links Free Pass

Chinese Investors Review Bond Holdings as Ratings Face Scrutiny
stories.summary: Chinese asset managers are reviewing their bond holdings to identify issuers at risk of rating downgrades after regulators stepped up efforts to curb the over-concentration of AAA ratings, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: With Final Decisions Ahead, the Supreme Court Is Sharply Divided

stories.headline: White House Releases Images of the Trump ‘Patriot Passport’
